prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and metabolic syndrome prevalence

vulnerablePopulations: patients with CKD and metabolic syndrome
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Crystal-proven diagnosis preferred; ultrasound/DECT supportive; EULAR-aligned criteria

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with treat-to-target urate strategies in many systems

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Access to imaging and rheumatology services varies but is generally high in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat-to-target urate lowering (allopurinol/febuxostat) with flare prophylaxis; lifestyle counseling; biologics for refractory cases in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Adherence and comorbidity management; variation across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: EULAR guideline-driven care and broad medication availability
